issues.description: Top navigation only shows Sessions and Settings tabs. The Help dialog lists 5 features (Sessions, Teams, Memory, Reports, Settings) but Teams, Memory, and Reports are missing from the nav bar. A user would never discover these features without using the command palette (Cmd+K). The Settings page says 'Agent System: Not configured - Teams and Memory tabs are hidden' but there's no way for a new user to know tabs are hidden or that they need an 'agent system' to unlock them.
issues.checklist_item: Does the Help dialog features list match the actual tabs in the navigation?

issues.description: The top bar shows '$209.6' as a bare number with no label, tooltip, or context. A new user has no idea what this cost refers to - is it total spend today? This month? All time? Per session? The number just sits there unexplained between the tabs and the system resource badge.
issues.checklist_item: Are all top bar indicators (usage, limits, peak hours) labeled or explained?

issues.description: The 'Limits' link in the top bar navigates away from the app to claude.ai, which requires Cloudflare verification. The user is taken completely out of their workflow with no warning. External links should open in new tabs and be labeled as external.
issues.checklist_item: Do all navigation elements respond to clicks? (no dead links)

issues.description: The 'API: Off-Peak' badge in the top bar has a tooltip explaining peak/off-peak hours, but the badge itself is confusing without context. A new user doesn't know what 'API: Off-Peak' means, what API it refers to, or why it matters. The tooltip helps but only appears on hover.
issues.checklist_item: Are all top bar indicators (usage, limits, peak hours) labeled or explained?

issues.description: Running on Machine section shows sessions labeled only by model name ('opus') with no session name, project, or task context. When multiple sessions use the same model, they are indistinguishable. Users cannot tell which session is doing what. Session names should be human-readable descriptions.
issues.checklist_item: Can I tell sessions apart from their names? (not all identical)

issues.description: Recent Sessions shows truncated prompt text as session identifiers (e.g., '# /loop - schedule a recurring or self-paced promp...'). These are unreadable, unhelpful, and many look identical. Session names should be meaningful summaries, not raw prompt excerpts.
issues.checklist_item: Are session names human-readable? (descriptive, not branch names or UUIDs)

issues.description: The General settings shows 'opus' as the selected default model, but the New Session launcher modal defaults to 'sonnet'. The Setup Wizard Preferences also shows 'sonnet' selected. The default model setting does not propagate to the session launcher, making the setting useless.
issues.checklist_item: Can I set a default model for new sessions?

issues.description: Default Working Directory is set to '/Applications/Agent Studio.app/Contents/Resources' which is the Electron app bundle path, not a user project directory. This means sessions launched from the UI would run in the wrong directory. A new user who doesn't change this will have a broken experience.
issues.checklist_item: Can I configure workspace/project paths?

issues.description: The About section shows Branch as 'AGENTS_SETUP' but the actual git branch is 'feat/v030-room-reliability'. This value appears to be hardcoded or stale rather than dynamically read from git.
issues.checklist_item: Is there an About section with version number?

issues.description: Clicking 'Re-run Setup Wizard' in Settings triggers the wizard to persist across page reloads. Even navigating to localhost:8080 directly shows the wizard instead of the main app. The only way to get back to the app is to complete all wizard steps. There is no close/dismiss button on the wizard, and pressing Escape does not close it.
issues.checklist_item: Is there a setup wizard or getting-started flow for first-time users?

issues.description: Sessions cannot be renamed. The sidebar shows sessions by truncated prompt text or model name, and there is no way to give them meaningful names. The SESSION NAME field in the launcher is optional and its effect is unclear.
issues.checklist_item: Can I rename a session?

issues.description: No search or filter capability for recent sessions. With 20 recent sessions showing, users must visually scan the entire list to find a specific one. The truncated prompt text makes this even harder.
issues.checklist_item: Can I search/filter session history?

issues.description: The 'Kill process' buttons in Running on Machine section have no confirmation dialog. Clicking kill immediately terminates the process. The DESIGN.md specifically calls for 'Kill session? This can't be undone.' confirmation, and IDENTITY.md says destructive confirmation should be direct.
issues.checklist_item: Can I kill a session from the UI with confirmation?

issues.description: Teams/Rooms view is only accessible via the command palette (Cmd+K -> Teams / Sprint View). There is no tab or link in the main navigation even though the feature exists and is functional. A user would never discover this feature naturally.
issues.checklist_item: NEW

issues.description: Closing a room has no confirmation dialog. The DESIGN.md calls for confirmation before destroying resources. A misclick could destroy an active agent collaboration.
issues.checklist_item: Does closing a room show a confirmation dialog before destroying it?

issues.description: Sprints tab is nested inside the Teams view (accessible only via command palette) as a sub-tab. The DESIGN.md specifies it should be a prominent feature. It's buried two levels deep from the main navigation.
issues.checklist_item: NEW

issues.description: Memory tab is completely hidden when agent system is not configured. The command palette shows 'Memory Stats: 0 entries' but there is no Memory tab anywhere in the navigation. Even after the agent system is configured, there's no evidence the Memory tab would appear in the top nav. Users cannot browse, search, filter, create, edit, or delete memory entries.
issues.checklist_item: Can I see all memory entries?

issues.description: Notification toggle buttons (MAC/TG columns) are small and their on/off state is only distinguishable by color (amber=on, dark=off). There is no text label like 'On'/'Off' and the contrast between states is subtle. Users with color vision deficiency would struggle.
issues.checklist_item: Can I turn off Mac notifications entirely?

issues.description: Notifications section says 'Settings are local to this browser session. Server-side persistence coming soon.' This is a red flag for users - their carefully configured notification preferences will be lost when they close the browser. This should be more prominent or the feature should be server-persisted.
issues.checklist_item: NEW

issues.description: The Repos section shows a repo called 'staging-frontend' with branch 'staging/frontend' and action buttons (View Changes, Commit, Push, Create Pull Request). But there is no way to add a new repo from the sidebar - users must go to Settings > Workspace to add projects. The sidebar should have an 'Add Repo' action.
issues.checklist_item: Can I see git status for my repos?

issues.description: The Servers section shows 'agent-studio this app :8080' with the port clickable, but clicking :8080 does nothing visible. It should either open the URL in a browser or show a toast confirming the action.
issues.checklist_item: Can I see which port each server is on?

issues.description: The 'API: Off-Peak' badge wraps awkwardly when the viewport is reduced (e.g., during Cmd+Shift+F fullscreen toggle). The text breaks to two lines making it look broken. The badge should truncate or use a shorter label at narrow widths.
issues.checklist_item: Does the layout adjust gracefully when resizing?

issues.description: The 'Continue Last' preset button says 'Resume previous' but gives no indication of which session will be resumed. A user with 20 recent sessions has no way to know which one will be picked. The button should show the name or time of the last session.
issues.checklist_item: Can I resume a previous session from the UI?

issues.description: No per-session cost display in the sidebar session list. The top bar shows total cost but individual session cost is only visible when the Running on Machine section is expanded. Users need to see how much each session is costing at a glance.
issues.checklist_item: Is per-session cost visible in the sidebar session list (not just total in top bar)?

issues.description: PMO Scheduler section in Settings says 'Running' with a Stop button, but there is no explanation of what PMO stands for, what a scan does, or why a user would want one. A new user would be confused by jargon like 'PMO' and 'scan.'
issues.checklist_item: NEW

issues.description: The Settings page has no 'Automations' section despite this being mentioned in the task requirements. There is no way to configure automated workflows, scheduled tasks, or recurring actions beyond the PMO Scheduler.
issues.checklist_item: NEW

issues.description: The Settings tab button in the top nav shows a gear icon but inconsistently displays text. On the Sessions view it shows 'Settings' text, matching the design, but the icon-only state is inconsistent with how Sessions is always labeled.
issues.checklist_item: Can the user tell what each top-level tab does from its label alone?

issues.description: Running on Machine shows '3' processes but these are system-wide Claude processes, not sessions started from Agent Studio. The Sessions count shows '0'. This disconnect is confusing - why are there 3 running processes but 0 sessions? The relationship between 'Running on Machine' and 'Sessions' is not explained.
issues.checklist_item: Can I see ALL Claude Code sessions running on my machine?

issues.description: The Sprints sub-tab shows a 'WORKFLOWS' section with 'No workflows found' heading, but there is no explanation of what workflows are or how to create one. The 'New Sprint' dialog only creates sprints, not workflows. The workflow concept is introduced but not functional.
issues.checklist_item: NEW

issues.description: The Setup Wizard has no close/dismiss/skip button. Once triggered (either on first launch or via Re-run Setup Wizard), the user must complete all 4 steps. There is no escape hatch. Even pressing Escape or refreshing the page does not close it.
issues.checklist_item: NEW

issues.description: The system resources tooltip shows memory at 99% (23.8/24GB) which looks alarming. The red color bar reinforces this alarm. However, this is likely normal macOS memory management (disk cache counted as used). The UI should either explain this or only show 'application memory pressure' rather than raw usage.
issues.checklist_item: Can I see CPU/RAM/disk usage?

issues.description: No session grouping in the sidebar. The DESIGN.md specifies sessions should be grouped as 'SPRINT TEAM' (auto-detected agent team sessions) and 'STANDALONE' (ad-hoc chats). Currently all sessions appear in a flat list with no grouping.
issues.checklist_item: Are sessions grouped logically? (sprint team vs standalone)

issues.description: No Reports tab visible anywhere in the UI despite being listed in the Help dialog features. There is no way to view automation results, sprint reports, or any kind of reporting.
issues.checklist_item: NEW

issues.description: No loading states observed anywhere in the app. When clicking buttons that trigger actions (like View Changes on a repo), there is no loading indicator. The DESIGN.md spec calls for 'a thin amber line at the top of the loading panel' or skeleton states.
issues.checklist_item: NEW

issues.description: The IDENTITY.md specifies Geist Mono as the ONLY font, but the app appears to use a monospace font that may be system default rather than Geist Mono. Without a font inspector this is difficult to confirm visually, but the font rendering differs from typical Geist Mono.
issues.checklist_item: Is Geist Mono the ONLY font?

issues.description: The 'Create Agent System' button in Workspace settings is the gateway to unlocking Teams and Memory tabs, but it's buried in Settings. A new user who just wants to try the app would never find this. The empty state or onboarding flow should guide users to configure the agent system.
issues.checklist_item: If no teams/rooms exist: does it explain what teams are and how to start?

issues.description: The sidebar shows 'SERVERS 1' as a collapsed section header, but when expanded it actually shows 3 entries (agent-studio running on :8080, agent-studio with Start button, staging-frontend with Start button). The count '1' only reflects running servers, not total configured, which is confusing.
issues.checklist_item: NEW

issues.description: The Running on Machine section shows duplicate information: each entry has 'running 28m' shown both as a badge near the model and as text at the end. The duration appears twice per entry without adding value.
issues.checklist_item: NEW

summary: Agent Studio has a solid foundation with a clean dark UI, functional sidebar, working keyboard shortcuts, and a well-designed setup wizard. However, the app has serious discoverability problems: 3 of 5 advertised features (Teams, Memory, Reports) are hidden from the main navigation, accessible only via command palette or buried in settings. Session identification is poor -- processes show only 'opus' and recent sessions show truncated prompt text, making it impossible to distinguish sessions. The default working directory points to the Electron app bundle, which would break all sessions for new users. The setup wizard cannot be dismissed once triggered. Settings don't propagate correctly (default model is ignored by the session launcher). For a user who just found this on GitHub, the app looks like a terminal launcher with settings -- they would never discover the Teams, Sprints, Memory, or Reports features without reading documentation.
